knowledge:content-formulas:fab-formula.content_summary:
## 1. FAB Formula

**Meaning:** Highlight product/service value through features, advantages, and benefits.

### Structure

- **F – Features:** What the product/service has.
- **A – Advantages:** What makes it better than competitors.
- **B – Benefit...

knowledge:content-formulas:fas-formula.content_summary:
## 2. FAS Formula

**Meaning:** Convert pain points into purchase intent by showing a solution.

### Structure

- **F / P – Problem:** Identify customer pain point
- **A – Agitate:** Intensify the pain/problem
- **S – Solve:** Present the solution

#...

knowledge:content-formulas:bab-formula.content_summary:
## 3. BAB Formula

**Meaning:** Show transformation from before to after.

### Structure

- **B – Before:** Current painful state
- **A – After:** Desired future state
- **B – Bridge:** Your product/service bridges the gap

### Best Use Cases

- Beau...

knowledge:content-formulas:4c-formula.content_summary:
## 4. 4C Formula

**Meaning:** High-quality writing standards.

### Structure

- **Clear:** Easy to understand
- **Concise:** Short and direct
- **Compelling:** Persuasive and engaging
- **Credible:** Trustworthy with proof

### Best Use Cases

- All...

knowledge:content-formulas:4u-formula.content_summary:
## 5. 4U Formula

**Meaning:** Create irresistible headlines/hooks.

### Structure

- **Useful:** Valuable to audience
- **Urgent:** Time-sensitive or immediate need
- **Unique:** Different from others
- **Ultra-specific:** Specific and concrete

###...

knowledge:content-formulas:a-forest-form.content_summary:
## 6. A FOREST Formula

**Meaning:** Persuasive writing framework using rhetorical devices.

### Structure

- **A – Alliteration:** Repeated sounds
- **F – Facts:** Truth/data
- **O – Opinions:** Arguments/viewpoints
- **R – Repetition:** Repeat key ...

knowledge:content-formulas:aida-formula.content_summary:
## 7. AIDA Formula

**Meaning:** Classic conversion funnel copywriting model.

### Structure

- **A – Attention:** Get noticed
- **I – Interest:** Build curiosity
- **D – Desire:** Create want/need
- **A – Action:** Ask for action

### Best Use Cases...

knowledge:content-formulas:5-objections-.content_summary:
## 8. 5 Objections Formula

**Meaning:** Remove common buying resistance.

### Structure

- I don't have money
- I don't have time
- I don't like it
- I don't trust it
- I don't need it

### Best Use Cases

- Sales copy
- Objection handling posts
- F...

knowledge:content-formulas:3-reasons-why.content_summary:
## 9. 3 Reasons Why Formula

**Meaning:** Build logic and trust through 3 answers.

### Structure

- Why is your product the best?
- Why should I buy it?
- Why should I trust you?

### Best Use Cases

- Comparison ads
- Short-form video
- Product pag...

knowledge:content-formulas:4p-formula.content_summary:
## 10. 4P Formula

**Meaning:** Strong sales persuasion formula.

### Structure

- **Picture:** Show attractive vision/result
- **Promise:** Make clear promise
- **Prove:** Provide evidence
- **Push:** CTA / urge action

### Best Use Cases

- Faceboo...

knowledge:content-formulas:3s-formula.content_summary:
## 11. 3S Formula

**Meaning:** Story-based persuasion.

### Structure

- **Star:** Main character
- **Story:** Journey/conflict
- **Solution:** Resolution using your offer

### Best Use Cases

- Storytelling posts
- Founder stories
- Facebook posts
...

knowledge:content-formulas:4a-formula.content_summary:
## 12. 4A Formula

**Meaning:** Brand action loop model.

### Structure

- **Aware:** Know the brand
- **Attitude:** Build positive feeling
- **Act:** Purchase/action
- **Act Again:** Repeat purchase

### Best Use Cases

- Retention strategy
- Brand ...

knowledge:content-formulas:5a-formula.content_summary:
## 13. 5A Formula

**Meaning:** Modern customer journey model (Philip Kotler).

### Structure

- **Awareness:** Know brand
- **Appeal:** Feel attracted
- **Ask:** Research and compare
- **Action:** Buy
- **Advocate:** Recommend brand

### Best Use Ca...

knowledge:content-formulas:app-formula.content_summary:
## 14. APP Formula

**Meaning:** Fast hook structure for modern content.

### Structure

- **Agree:** Show you understand their problem
- **Promise:** Promise value/solution
- **Preview:** Explain what they'll get

### Best Use Cases

- TikTok intros...

knowledge:content-formulas:platform-mapp.content_summary:
## Platform Mapping TikTok

### Primary Formulas

- 4U
- AIDA
- APP
- BAB
- 3 Reasons Why

### Secondary Formulas

- FAS
- 3S
- FAB

### Best For

- Hooks
- Retention
- Fast education
- Viral short-form content

---

## Platform Mapping Facebook Post

### Primary Formulas

- BAB
- AIDA
- FAB
- 3S
- 5 Objections

### Secondary Formulas

- 4P
- FAS
- APP
- 4C
- 3 Reasons Why

### Best For

- Storytelling
- Community engagement
- Sales posts
- Trust building

---

## Platform Mapping Facebook Reel

### Primary Formulas

- 4U
- AIDA
- APP
- BAB
- 3 Reasons Why

### Secondary Formulas

- FAS
- 3S

### Best For

- Relatable short videos
- Product demo reels
- Fast conversions

---

## Platform Mapping Instagram Post

### Primary Formulas

- 4C
- FAB
- 4P
- 5A
- APP

### Secondary Formulas

- AIDA
- BAB
- FAS
- 3 Reasons Why

### Best For

- Carousel education
- Brand identity
- Saveable content
- Product showcase

---

## Platform Mapping Instagram Reel

### Primary Formulas

- 4U
- AIDA
- APP
- BAB
- 3 Reasons Why

### Secondary Formulas

- FAS
- 3S
- FAB

### Best For

- Aesthetic reels
- Tips
- Transformation content
- Growth content

---

## Platform Mapping Threads Post

### Primary Formulas

- 4U
- APP
- BAB
- 3S
- 3 Reasons Why

### Secondary Formulas

- AIDA
- 4C
- FAS

### Best For

- Opinions
- Relatable thoughts
- Mini storytelling
- Community discussions

---

knowledge:content-formulas:category-cros.content_summary:
## Category Cross-Platform High Performance

Use these formulas across any platform when you want maximum reach with minimum platform-specific tuning:

- 4U
- AIDA
- BAB
- APP
- FAB

---

knowledge:content-formulas:category-hook.content_summary:
## Category Hook Viral

When the goal is to grab attention in the first 1–3 seconds:

- 4U
- APP
- 3 Reasons Why

---

knowledge:content-formulas:category-stor.content_summary:
## Category Storytelling Trust

When the goal is to build trust through narrative or vulnerability:

- BAB
- 3S

---

knowledge:content-formulas:category-conv.content_summary:
## Category Conversion Sales

When the goal is to drive purchase or signup:

- AIDA
- FAB
- FAS
- 4P
- 5 Objections

---

knowledge:content-formulas:category-bran.content_summary:
## Category Branding Audience Growth

When the goal is to compound brand recognition over time:

- 4C
- 4A
- 5A

---

knowledge:content-formulas:category-low-.content_summary:
## Category Low Priority Short Social

Avoid these in short-form social content (too long-form / too strategic):

- A FOREST
- 4A
- 5A (except brand strategy or Instagram post)

knowledge:engagement-triggers:save-bait-.content_summary:
## Save-bait vs Share-bait vs Comment-bait

**Meaning:** Each engagement type rewards a different content style. Design the post for the action you want.

### When to use

- Anytime — pick the goal first, then the format
- Don't try to optimize for a...

knowledge:engagement-triggers:question-e.content_summary:
## Question-Ending

**Meaning:** End every post with a specific, easy-to-answer question to drive comments.

### When to use

- Carousel posts, long captions, threads
- Anytime you want to boost the comment count

### Example

- Bad: "Let me know wha...

knowledge:engagement-triggers:vulnerabil.content_summary:
## Vulnerability Posts

**Meaning:** Share a personal struggle, mistake, or admission that contradicts the polished social-media norm.

### When to use

- Personal brand accounts
- Coaches, founders, creators building thought leadership
- Sparingly —...

knowledge:engagement-triggers:controvers.content_summary:
## Controversial-but-Positive

**Meaning:** Take an opinion that splits your audience without being mean. The goal is debate, not trolling.

### When to use

- Established accounts with a defined audience
- Topics where conventional wisdom is weak

#...

knowledge:engagement-triggers:insider-kn.content_summary:
## Insider Knowledge

**Meaning:** Frame content as "what nobody tells you" or "industry secrets". Implies the reader is now in-the-know.

### When to use

- Niche-specific educational content
- Saveable carousels and threads
- Audiences hungry for s...

knowledge:engagement-triggers:listicle-c.content_summary:
## Listicle / Carousel Anatomy

**Meaning:** Structure a multi-slide post as: hook slide + 4–7 value slides + CTA slide. Each slide must earn the next swipe.

### When to use

- Educational content
- Instagram and Facebook carousels
- LinkedIn slides...

knowledge:engagement-triggers:algorithm-.content_summary:
## Algorithm-Friendly Cadence

**Meaning:** Posting frequency that the platform's ranker rewards without flooding your audience.

### Guidance

- **Instagram:** 1–2 posts/day max in feed, plus 2–4 stories. Posting more often dilutes reach per post.
-...

knowledge:engagement-triggers:golden-hou.content_summary:
## Golden Hour

**Meaning:** The first 60 minutes after posting determines the post's overall reach. Maximize early engagement.

### When to use

- Every post — but most aggressively for content you've invested heavily in

### Tactics

- Post when yo...

knowledge:image-design-branded:when-to-u.content_summary:
## When To Use Branded Style

**Meaning:** Default for everyday posts. Pick this when neither pure-vibe (creative) nor sales-flyer (marketing) is right.

### Best fit
- Tip / advice posts ("3 things you should know about X")
- News / updates / announ...

knowledge:image-design-branded:branded-c.content_summary:
## Branded Composition Rules

**Meaning:** Strong photographic / illustrative composition first, brand layer second.

### Apply
- **One hero subject** plus space for one short headline
- **Headline-safe area:** Top third or bottom third of the frame ...

knowledge:image-design-branded:branded-t.content_summary:
## Branded Text Rules

**Meaning:** One short headline, optionally one short subhead. Never a full text block, never CTAs, never contact info.

### Apply
- **One headline** of 3–8 words. Examples: *"Your camera, smarter."* / *"Trick #2: light from th...

knowledge:image-design-branded:branded-b.content_summary:
## Branded Brand Presence

**Meaning:** Brand mark is visible but does not dominate.

### Apply
- **Wordmark or logo** in one corner (top-left or bottom-right preferred), 6–10% of frame width
- **Brand color accent** somewhere in the frame — a stripe...

knowledge:image-design-branded:branded-c.content_summary:
## Branded Color & Lighting

**Meaning:** Pull palette + lighting from the brand identity (RAG'd from past posts). Branded posts should look like they belong in the same grid as everything else.

### Apply
- **Palette:** Pull primary color from page ...

knowledge:image-design-branded:branded-l.content_summary:
## Branded Layout Patterns

**Meaning:** Three reliable patterns work for ~80% of branded posts. Use one, do not invent.

### Apply

**Pattern A — Top-Title Photo**
- Top third: brand color or white block + headline
- Bottom two-thirds: hero photo
- ...

knowledge:image-design-creative:when-to-.content_summary:
## When To Use Creative Style

**Meaning:** Pick this style when the goal is brand vibe, emotional connection, or mood — not conversion or education.

### Best fit
- Behind-the-scenes glimpses, team moments, brand storytelling
- Lifestyle / aspiratio...

knowledge:image-design-creative:creative.content_summary:
## Creative Composition Rules

**Meaning:** Treat the image like a single editorial photograph. One subject, strong composition, atmosphere over information.

### Apply
- **Single hero subject** — one clear focal point, not a crowded layout
- **Rule ...

## Creative Text Rules

**Meaning:** The image should be readable WITHOUT text. Any text on the image is a constraint, not a feature.

### Apply
- **No text by default.** The caption carries copy
- **Allowed exceptions:** A single short word/phrase a...

## Creative Color & Mood

**Meaning:** Palette is dictated by the brand identity from the page profile, not invented per post.

### Apply
- **Pull palette from past posts** — use the RAG'd reference images to lock palette
- **2–3 colors max** — limit...

## Creative Brand Presence

**Meaning:** Brand is felt, not displayed. The viewer should know it is your brand from the visual style alone.

### Apply
- **No on-image logo** by default. If the brand logo is included, it is small (under 8% of frame), ...

knowledge:image-design-marketing:when-to.content_summary:
## When To Use Marketing Style

**Meaning:** Pick this style when the post has a specific commercial goal and the image must close the loop on its own.

### Best fit
- Product launches, new arrivals
- Sales / discounts / limited-time offers
- Service...

knowledge:image-design-marketing:marketi.content_summary:
## Marketing Composition Rules

**Meaning:** Layout for fast comprehension. The viewer decides "is this for me?" in under one second.

### Apply
- **F-pattern reading order.** Top-left → top-right → middle → bottom-right (CTA goes bottom-right)
- **V...

## Marketing Text Rules

**Meaning:** This is the only style where the image carries multiple text layers. Hierarchy must be obvious at a glance.

### Apply

**Headline (mandatory)**
- 3–6 words maximum
- The value proposition or the offer ("50% off ...

## Marketing Brand Presence

**Meaning:** Brand must be unmistakable. Logo + name + colors + tagline if present.

### Apply
- **Brand logo lockup** — full logo with wordmark, top-left or paired with the CTA. 12–18% of frame width
- **Brand colors enf...

## Marketing Visual Anchors

**Meaning:** The hero visual must support the offer, not distract from it.

### Apply
- **Product shot:** clean background, single product, well-lit, NOT a busy lifestyle scene
- **Before/after:** split-frame, clear label...

## Marketing Color & Contrast

**Meaning:** High contrast wins clicks. Subtlety loses to noise in the feed.

### Apply
- **High-contrast text-to-background** — minimum WCAG AAA (7:1) for headlines
- **CTA button must POP** — pick the color with highe...

## Marketing Trust Signals (Optional Add-Ons)

**Meaning:** Marketing posts win on credibility. Sprinkle one or two signals if they fit.

### Apply (pick at most 2)
- **Social proof:** "★★★★★ 4.9/5 from 2,400 reviews"
- **Scarcity:** "Only 12 left" /...

knowledge:platform-algorithm-signals:fac.content_summary:
## Facebook

**Primary signals:** Meaningful interactions > watch time > engagement velocity > recency

### What the ranker rewards

- **Meaningful interactions:** Comments and replies count more than likes. Long comments count more than short ones. ...

knowledge:platform-algorithm-signals:ins.content_summary:
## Instagram

**Primary signals:** Saves > Shares > Comments > Likes; for Reels: watch-loops + completion

### What the ranker rewards

- **Saves:** The strongest signal in the feed algorithm. Carousels are heavily favored because they generate more ...

knowledge:platform-algorithm-signals:tik.content_summary:
## TikTok

**Primary signals:** Completion rate > rewatches > shares > comments > likes; first 3-second retention is critical

### What the ranker rewards

- **Completion rate:** Percentage of viewers who watched to the end. The single most important...

knowledge:platform-algorithm-signals:thr.content_summary:
## Threads

**Primary signals:** Reply-rate > quote-replies > likes; trending-topic association

### What the ranker rewards

- **Reply-rate:** How many viewers reply to the post (vs just liking). The dominant signal — Threads is conversation-first.
...

knowledge:platform-algorithm-signals:you.content_summary:
## YouTube (for future expansion)

**Primary signals:** Click-through rate (CTR) on thumbnail + average view duration; first-30-second retention

### What the ranker rewards

- **CTR:** Thumbnail + title combination must earn the click
- **Average vi...

knowledge:viral-hooks:3-second-rule.content_summary:
## 3-Second Rule

**Meaning:** Deliver a visual + emotional payoff before the viewer's thumb reaches to scroll.

### When to use

- Reels, TikTok, Stories, Shorts — any short-form video
- Any time you need to stop the scroll on autoplay feeds

### Ex...

knowledge:viral-hooks:pattern-interrupt.content_summary:
## Pattern Interrupt

**Meaning:** Open with something the viewer didn't expect — a contradiction, a controversial take, an abrupt cut, or a visual that breaks feed convention.

### When to use

- Saturated topics where most posts look alike
- Audien...

knowledge:viral-hooks:curiosity-gap.content_summary:
## Curiosity Gap

**Meaning:** Tease a piece of information without revealing it, forcing the viewer to keep watching to close the loop.

### When to use

- Listicle content ("3 things I wish I knew…")
- Behind-the-scenes / reveal videos
- Tutorial c...

knowledge:viral-hooks:open-loop.content_summary:
## Open Loop

**Meaning:** Pose a specific question at the start, answer it at the end. Variant of Curiosity Gap, but more disciplined.

### When to use

- Educational reels and threads
- Story-based posts
- Long-ish (30s+) videos where retention is ...

knowledge:viral-hooks:before-after-tease.content_summary:
## Before/After Tease

**Meaning:** Show the transformation upfront in a single frame or split-second cut — viewer wants to know HOW.

### When to use

- Fitness, beauty, finance, productivity, design
- Anything with a visible result
- Carousel post ...

knowledge:viral-hooks:high-contrast-cold.content_summary:
## High-Contrast Cold Open

**Meaning:** Open with a visual or audio shock — bright color flash, sudden silence after loud audio, or vice versa.

### When to use

- Reels and TikToks where attention competes with autoplay neighbors
- Brand content ne...

knowledge:viral-hooks:direct-address.content_summary:
## Direct Address

**Meaning:** Call out the specific role / situation of your target viewer in the first sentence so they know it's for them.

### When to use

- Niche content (B2B, specific industries, lifestyle micro-niches)
- Educational posts wi...

knowledge:viral-hooks:counterintuitive-c.content_summary:
## Counterintuitive Claim

**Meaning:** Open with a statement that challenges a widely-held assumption in your niche.

### When to use

- Thought-leadership content
- Threads / LinkedIn-style posts
- Long-form caption posts

### Example

"You don't n...

knowledge:viral-hooks:numbered-promise.content_summary:
## Numbered Promise

**Meaning:** Lead with a specific number + concrete outcome. Specificity outperforms vague claims.

### When to use

- Listicle / educational content
- Carousel slide 1 / Reel cover text
- Threads opening posts
- Blog/email subje...

knowledge:viral-hooks:stakes-first.content_summary:
## Stakes-First

**Meaning:** Open with what the viewer will lose by NOT watching, rather than what they'll gain by watching. Loss-aversion is roughly 2x more motivating than equivalent gain.

### When to use

- High-cost decisions (financial, career...

knowledge:visual-design:rule-of-thirds.content_summary:
## Rule of Thirds

**Meaning:** Divide the frame into a 3×3 grid. Place the subject and key elements on the gridlines or their intersections, not centered.

### When to use

- Photo composition for feed posts
- Image-generation prompts (specify "subj...

knowledge:visual-design:f-pattern-readin.content_summary:
## F-Pattern Reading

**Meaning:** On text-heavy images, the eye scans in an F shape — top horizontal sweep, second horizontal sweep below, then vertical scan down the left side.

### When to use

- Carousel slides with body text
- Quote graphics
- I...

knowledge:visual-design:mobile-first-9-1.content_summary:
## Mobile-First (9:16)

**Meaning:** Reels, Stories, TikTok, and Shorts default to vertical full-screen 9:16. Design with that aspect ratio in mind from the start.

### When to use

- All short-form video
- Story content
- IGTV / FB Reels / YouTube S...

knowledge:visual-design:square-1-1-for-f.content_summary:
## Square (1:1) for Feed

**Meaning:** Standard Instagram and Facebook feed posts default to 1:1 square (or 4:5 portrait). Don't repurpose 16:9 horizontal for feed.

### When to use

- Instagram feed posts
- Facebook feed posts
- Carousel slides

###...

knowledge:visual-design:text-overlay-rul.content_summary:
## Text Overlay Rules

**Meaning:** Text on images/video must be readable in 1 second on a small screen.

### When to use

- Reel cover text
- Carousel slide headlines
- Any image with overlaid copy

### Tactics

- **Contrast:** Text color must contr...

knowledge:visual-design:brand-color-disc.content_summary:
## Brand Color Discipline

**Meaning:** Use 2–3 colors maximum, repeated consistently across every post.

### When to use

- All brand and creator accounts
- Especially important for new accounts building recognition

### Tactics

- Pick: 1 dominant ...

knowledge:visual-design:font-pairing.content_summary:
## Font Pairing

**Meaning:** Use one display font (for headlines) and one body font (for everything else). No more.

### When to use

- All text-on-image content
- Carousel slides
- Reel text overlays

### Tactics

- **Display:** A bold, geometric s...

knowledge:visual-design:carousel-pacing.content_summary:
## Carousel Pacing

**Meaning:** Each slide must earn the next swipe. Treat slides like cliffhanger episodes.

### When to use

- All multi-slide posts (Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn)

### Structure

- **Slide 1:** Hook — bold visual + numbered promi...

knowledge:visual-design:thumbnail-optimi.content_summary:
## Thumbnail Optimization

**Meaning:** Your post is judged at thumbnail size (200px wide on most feeds) before anyone reads it. Design for that size first.

### When to use

- Reel covers
- Carousel slide 1
- Static feed images

### Tactics

- Test ...

knowledge:visual-design:negative-space.content_summary:
## Negative Space

**Meaning:** Let the subject breathe. Resist the urge to fill every pixel with information.

### When to use

- All visual design
- Especially photo posts and minimal-text quote graphics

### Tactics

- Aim for 30–50% of the frame ...
